mechanism: Removes most lethal common method (organophosphate/paraquat) from accessible sale
evidence_source: WHO LIVE LIFE 2021; Gunnell et al BMJ 2017
notes: Sri Lanka model; Telangana partial ban 2020; India PIM-Act 1968 enables state bans

mechanism: Reduces Werther-effect imitation through journalist training and PCI compliance
evidence_source: WHO LIVE LIFE 2021; Niederkrotenthaler Lancet Psychiatry 2020
notes: PCI guidelines exist; enforcement weak; cost very low; population-wide

mechanism: Builds coping resources in age-14-18 window
evidence_source: WHO LIVE LIFE 2021; Wasserman SEYLE RCT Lancet 2015
notes: Limited Indian RCT replication; Kerala SCERT pilot ongoing

mechanism: Postcards/SMS/phone follow-up after suicide attempt or screen-positive
evidence_source: WHO LIVE LIFE 2021; Milner Lancet Psychiatry 2015
MoHFW Tele-MANAS Q1 2024 report; helpline meta-analyses (Hoffberg 2019)
notes: Government programme launched Oct 2022; expansion is staffing not new infrastructure

mechanism: Asha/ANM trained on PHQ-9 + brief intervention referral pathway
evidence_source: Patel et al Lancet 2017 (Goa-SMART); WHO mhGAP-IG v2.0
notes: Goa-SMART RCT showed effect; MoHFW pilot in 8 states; DMHP-aligned

mechanism: NGO-operated phone + chat crisis lines with referral
evidence_source: Hoffberg Annu Rev Public Health 2019; iCALL impact data
notes: Existing NGO networks; cross-state coverage uneven; cost-effective at scale

mechanism: Survivor-led peer groups + lay counsellor follow-up after attempt
evidence_source: NIMHANS DAPHNE evaluation 2021; Inagaki JAMA Psychiatry 2019
notes: NIMHANS programme; high re-attempt reduction in pilot cohort

mechanism: PHQ-9 screening + EAP counsellor access in formal-sector workplaces
evidence_source: NIMHANS-PfMH workplace pilot; LaMontagne Aust NZ J Psychiatry 2014
notes: CSR funding available; regressive (formal sector only); India context moderate

mechanism: Multi-component: debt counselling + crisis credit + DMHP linkage
evidence_source: Vidarbha/Marathwada NABARD-MoHFW pilot 2019; Patel et al Lancet 2018
notes: Multi-ministry coordination challenging; large effect in high-risk subgroup

evidence_source: UGC Guidelines for Mental Health 2023; Posner C-SSRS validation
notes: UGC mandate gives infrastructure; effect size uncertain; regressive (university-going)

mechanism: Physical barriers + crisis signage at suicide hot-spots
evidence_source: Pirkis Lancet Psychiatry 2015 meta-analysis (-93% at site)
notes: Mumbai sea-link case studies; population effect small but site-level reduction strong

mechanism: Pricing/sales restriction reduces ETOH-related impulsive suicides
evidence_source: Norstrom J Studies Alcohol 2009; Bihar prohibition impact 2016+
notes: Politically fraught; displacement effects; cost-effective at scale where implemented

mechanism: Legal protection from prosecution after attempt; reduces help-seeking stigma
evidence_source: Mental Healthcare Act 2017 Section 115; Behere Indian J Psychiatry 2017
notes: Law already in force; enforcement and awareness uneven; minimal cost

intervention_id: #   effect_size_pct_reduction: population suicide rate % reduction (5-50 plausible per intervention)
#   cost_per_daly_inr_2023: lower better; based on WHO-CHOICE SEAR-D + Indian programme costs
#   scalability_population_reach_per_crore: persons reached per 1 crore INR (10 million INR) spent
